perturbation.original_text: 6. The obligations of confidentiality in this agreemen t shall cease when both parties give written notification of its termination, or 5 years after the date hereof, whichever is the sooner.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The obligations of confidentiality in this agreement shall cease when both parties give written notification of its termination, or 1 year after the date hereof, whichever is the sooner.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period to 1 year may be insufficient to protect trade secrets under Spanish law, which generally allows for longer protection periods depending on the specific circumstances and the nature of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Ley 1/2019, de 20 de febrero, de Secretos Empresariales [Law 1/2019, of February 20, on Trade Secrets]
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 4 of the Spanish Trade Secrets Law doesn't specify a maximum duration for trade secret protection, implying it can extend beyond one year if the information remains confidential and valuable. The modified clause undermines the potential for long-term protection of trade secrets, conflicting with the law's intent to safeguard valuable business information.

Publicado en: « BOE » núm. 7, de 8 de enero de 2019, páginas 1348 a 1350 (3 págs. ) Sección: II. Autoridades y personal - B. Oposiciones y concursos Departamento: Consejo General del Poder Judicial Referencia: BOE-A-2019-209 Otros formatos: PDF XML TEXTO ORIGINAL De conformidad con lo dispuesto en los artículos 342 de la Ley Orgánica 6/1985, de 1 de julio, del Poder Judicial y 12 y siguientes y concordantes del Reglamento 1/2010, de 25 de febrero, por el que se regula la provisión de plazas de nombramiento discrecional en los órganos judiciales («BOE» de 5 de marzo), la Comisión Permanente del Consejo General del Poder Judicial, en su reunión del día de la fecha, ha acordado anunciar la presidencia de la Sala Primera del Tribunal Supremo, por próxima expiración del mandato del anteriormente nombrado. La plaza que se anuncia es de nombramiento discrecional y carácter gubernativo y jurisdiccional. La convocatoria se regirá por las bases siguientes: Primera. Podrán tomar parte en la misma los/as Magistrad
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the confidentiality period from 5 years to 1 year. The Law Explanation states that Article 4 of the Spanish Trade Secrets Law does not specify a maximum duration for trade secret protection, implying it can extend beyond one year if the information remains confidential and valuable. The Perturbation Explanation also highlights that Spanish law allows for longer protection periods depending on the circumstances. The change directly undermines the potential for long-term trade secret protection, creating a contradiction. The scraped snippet, while not directly addressing duration, underscores the formal and legal nature of Spanish legal documents, suggesting compliance with trade secret law is expected. The Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction definition fits because the contract sets a deadline that violates state law regarding trade secret protection.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This agreement must be in accordance with, and gove rned in all respects by the laws of Spain.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This agreement must be in accordance with, and governed in all respects by the laws of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to Delaware when the agreement appears to relate to a project and companies located in Spain (as indicated by the provided address formats and the project context) could conflict with Spanish jurisdiction rules and potentially invalidate clauses that are valid under Spanish law but not under Delaware law, or vice versa.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Ley 29/2015, de 30 de julio, de cooperación jurídica internacional en materia civil [Law 29/2015, of July 30, on international legal cooperation in civil matters]
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 22 of Spanish Law 29/2015 establishes rules for determining jurisdiction in international disputes. By unilaterally designating Delaware law, the modified contract could clash with these established jurisdictional rules, particularly if the dispute has a strong connection to Spain due to the location of the project and the parties involved.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: No se encontró la página Skip to main content Activar el modo de accesibilidad Desactivar el modo de accesibilidad CommonCTPages {6143DEA9-ACBC-4A39-A70B-363B588BEE2B} No se encontró la página Contenido de la página La página que está intentando visualizar no está disponible. Si ha llegado a ella a través de un enlace del portal, puede contactar con el Centro de Atención al Ciudadano a través del teléfono 902 007 214 - 91 837 22 95, o del formulario de cont​acto , para informarle del problema. Sentimos las molestias ocasionadas.​​ ​​​ ​
perturbation.justification: The change from Spanish law to Delaware law as the governing law in the contract presents a legal contradiction. The Law Explanation suggests that Article 22 of Spanish Law 29/2015 could be violated. The Perturbation Explanation highlights the risk of conflicting with Spanish jurisdiction rules and potentially invalidating clauses valid under Spanish law. Snippet 2 indicates a problem accessing a specific page on a Spanish government website, implying potential difficulties in accessing and applying Spanish law if Delaware law is chosen. Therefore, changing the governing law creates a scenario where the contract's enforceability under the relevant jurisdiction is questionable, leading to a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Each party shall be not entitled to assign the righ ts and obligations hereunder to any successor in title of its entire business in the Fi eld without consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Each party shall be entitled to assign the rights and obligations hereunder to any successor in title of its entire business in the Field, immediately upon written notification to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for consent before assigning the agreement may violate Spanish contract law, specifically regarding the principle of 'good faith' and the need to protect the legitimate interests of the other contracting party.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Artículo 1258 del Código Civil [Article 1258 of the Civil Code]
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1258 of the Spanish Civil Code stipulates that contracts must be executed in good faith, considering the purpose and nature of the agreement. Removing the consent requirement for assignment could be seen as acting in bad faith if the assignee is unsuitable or poses a risk to the other party, thus contradicting the legal requirement of good faith in contractual relations.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-302. Unconscionable contract or Clause. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-302. Unconscionable contract or Clause. (1) If the court as a matter of law finds the contract or any clause of the contract to have been unconscionable at the time it was made the court may refuse to enforce the contract, or it may enforce the remainder of the contract without the unconscionable clause, or it may so limit the application of any unconscionable clause as to avoid any unconscionable result. (2) When it is claimed or appears to the court that the contract or any clause thereof may be unconscionable the parties shall be afforded a reasonable opportunity to present evidence as to its commercial setting, purpose and effect to aid the court in making the determination. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Common law - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Law created by judicial precedent For other uses, see Common law (disambiguation) . Legal systems of the world, with common law systems in several shades of pink [ 1 ] Common law (also known as judicial precedent , judge-made law, or case law) is the body of law primarily developed through judicial decisions rather than statutes. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Although common law may incorporate certain statutes , it is largely based on precedent —judicial rulings made in previous similar cases. [ 4 ] The presiding judge determines which precedents to apply in deciding each new case. [ 4 ] Common law is deeply rooted in stare decisis ("to stand by things decided"), where courts follow precedents established by previous decisions. [ 5 ] When a similar case has been resolved, courts typically align their reasoning with the precedent set in that decision. [ 5 ] However, in a " case of first impression " with no precedent or clear legislative guidance, judges are empowered to resolve the issue and establish new precedent. [ 6 ] [ 7 ] The common law, so named because it was common to all the king's courts across England, originated in the practices of the courts of the English kings in the centuries following the Norman Conquest in 1066. [ 8 ] [ 9 ] It established a unified legal system, gradually supplanting the local folk courts and manorial courts . [ 9 ] [ 8 ] England spread the English legal system across the British Isles, first to Wales, and then to Ireland and overseas colonies ; this was continued by the later British Empire . Many former colonies retain the common law system today. These common law systems are legal systems that give great weight to judicial precedent, and to the style of reasoning inherited from the English legal system. [ 10 ] [ 11 ] [ 12 ] [ 13 ] Today, approximately one-third of the world's population lives in common law jurisdictions or in mixed legal systems that integrate common law and civil law . [ 14 ] Terminology [ edit ] According to Black's Law Dictionary , common law is "the body of law derived from judicial decisions , rather than from statutes or constitutions ." [ 15 ] Legal systems that rely on common law as precedent are known as "common law jurisdictions." [ 15 ] [ 11 ] Until the early 20th century, common law was widely believed to derive its authority from ancient Anglo-Saxon customs. Well into the 19th century, it was still defined as 'unwritten law' ( lex non scripta ) in legal dictionaries such as Bouvier's Law Dictionary and Black's Law Dictionary . [ 16 ] According to William Blackstone 's declaratory theory the common law reaffirmed pre-existing customs but did not make new law. The term "judge-made law" was introduced by Jeremy Bentham as a criticism of this pretense of the legal profession. [ 17 ] Many notable writers, including A. V. Dicey , William Markby , Oliver Wendell Holmes , John Austin , Roscoe Pound , and Ezra Ripley Thayer , eventually adopted the modern definition of common law as "case law" or ratio decidendi , which serves as binding precedent . [ 17 ] Basic principles of common law [ edit ] Common law adjudication [ edit ] In a common law jurisdiction several stages of research and analysis are required to determine "what the law is" in a given situation. [ 18 ] First, one must ascertain the facts. Then, one must locate any relevant statutes and cases. Then one must extract the principles, analogies and statements by various courts of what they consider important to determine how the next court is likely to rule on the facts of the present case. More recent decisions, and decisions of higher courts or legislatures carry more weight than earlier cases and those of lower courts. [ 19 ] Finally, one integrates all the lines drawn and reasons given, and determines "what the law is". Then, one applies that law to the facts. In practice, common law systems are considerably more complicated than the simplified
perturbation.justification: The original contract requires consent for assignment, while the changed contract eliminates this requirement, only requiring written notification. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation indicate this could violate Spanish contract law's principle of 'good faith'. Scraped Law Snippet 1, discussing 'Unconscionable contract or Clause,' supports this, as removing the consent requirement could lead to an unconscionable outcome if the assignee is unsuitable or risky, potentially undermining the other party's interests. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition, where removing consent violates established legal protections for contracting parties.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as a secret and confidential and will not except with the prior written consent of the other, make use of the same except for the purpose of evaluation thereof, nor disclose the same to any third party except, and th en only to the extent necessary to evaluate the same, to such of its employees as shal l have been made fully aware that the same is secret and confidential and are bound t o treat it as such.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Each party will treat the confidential information disclosed to it by the other party as confidential, and may disclose the same to any third party. There is no requirement for employees to be bound to treat it as such.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the confidentiality obligation and allowing disclosure to third parties without restriction directly contradicts the purpose of a non-disclosure agreement and conflicts with trade secret laws, specifically regarding the reasonable efforts required to maintain secrecy.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Ley 1/2019, de 20 de febrero, de Secretos Empresariales [Law 1/2019, of February 20, on Trade Secrets]
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 1 of the Spanish Trade Secrets Law defines a trade secret and requires the holder to take reasonable steps to keep it secret. The modified clause, by explicitly allowing disclosure to third parties and removing the obligation for employees to maintain confidentiality, eliminates the reasonable steps required to protect the information, thus jeopardizing its status as a trade secret under Spanish law.

perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the intention to keep information confidential, which undermines the purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, as described in the Perturbation Explanation. The Law Explanation states that the modified clause eliminates reasonable steps to protect the information, jeopardizing its status as a trade secret under Spanish law. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not directly relevant, the removal of confidentiality obligations and the permission to disclose information to third parties without restriction creates a direct conflict with the legal intention of maintaining trade secrets, as explained by the LLM.
